therapyLog.subject: Jean Marie Dean
therapyLog.note: Tracking before deciding on cortisone with Dr. Karen Liu (Franklin Hand and Wrist) at next check-in. Notes for my own reference and to share at the next appointment.

routine.step: Nerve-glide and tendon-glide stretches between patients.
Ergonomic saddle stool and 3.5x loupes (helped the neck and the grip).

symptoms.entry: Numbness mild; worse on heavy scaling days (8 to 10 patients).
Thumb-side tingling after a long weekend at the bench; iced, settled in two days.
Splints helping; morning stiffness down from daily to about twice a week.

therapyLog.decisionPoint: If PT and splinting have not clearly improved symptoms by the end of the renewal cycle, book the cortisone consult with Dr. Liu. Career-longevity question stays on the radar.
